prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
The last keystroke lands like a sparrow on still snow—  
no echo, no flutter, just hush.  
I peel my shadow off the chair,  
watch paragraphs rise and orbit the room,  
a stunned galaxy suddenly知道自己 free.  
The clock exhales a second it no longer needs.  
Outside, the moon is a white page turned blank.  
I am lighter than the space between stars,  
heavier than the silence I now own,  
a pilgrim who reaches the summit  
only to find the mountain has already walked away.
